name: Google Assistant
short_description: Google's conversational voice assistant that spans phones, smart speakers, displays, wearables, TVs, and cars.
long_description: Google Assistant is a multimodal, voice-first AI platform announced at Google I/O in 2016 that lets people ask questions, run tasks, and control connected devices through natural conversation. Rather than living in a single device, it is an ambient layer that follows the user across phones (Android, iOS), Nest speakers and displays, Wear OS watches, Android TV, and select cars. For physical AI in daily life, it is a foundational example of disembodied intelligence distributed through the home and on the body, shaping how millions interact with smart-home hardware by voice.

images.caption: Google Assistant running on a Google Pixel XL smartphone.
images.alt_text: A Google Pixel XL smartphone held in a hand, its screen showing the Google Assistant conversation interface with suggested prompts.

sources.title: Google unveils Google Assistant, a big upgrade to Google Now
sources.access_notes: Coverage of the I/O 2016 announcement establishing the 2016 launch year.

interactions.title: Wake the house with two words
interactions.description: Say "Hey Google, good morning" and a chained Routine fades up the lights, reads the weather and your calendar, and starts the news — one phrase standing in for a dozen taps.

interactions.title: Borrow a translator at the front desk
interactions.description: Tell it "be my Spanish interpreter" and it relays a back-and-forth conversation in real time across dozens of languages, speaking each side aloud (and showing text on a display).

interactions.title: Shout that dinner's ready
interactions.description: "Hey Google, broadcast that breakfast is ready" plays your recorded voice on every speaker and display in the house at once, and whoever's upstairs can talk back — an intercom built out of the smart-speaker mesh.

interactions.title: Rewarded for saying please
interactions.description: With Pretty Please switched on, a kid who tacks "please" onto a command gets a warm "thanks for asking so nicely" back — the assistant nudging household manners, which some parents loved and others found unsettling.

interactions.title: A night-time watch for a caregiver
interactions.description: In a dementia-care field study, a carer paired motion sensors with the assistant so it would announce when her husband got out of bed at night — turning an ambient speaker into an early-warning system she trusted more than checking the room.
interactions.source_label: PMC — voice assistants in cognitive-impairment care

interactions.title: Listening when nobody asked
interactions.description: Sounds that merely resemble "Hey Google" wake the mic and capture snippets of private conversation; the pattern of these false triggers drew a $68M U.S. privacy settlement in 2026.

interactions.title: A standing memory for the family
interactions.description: Ask it to remember where you parked, the wifi password, or a kid's shoe size, then pull any of it back from whichever device is nearest — leaning on the cross-device memory and Memory-aid role it already carries.

interactions.title: Hands-free coach mid-task
interactions.description: Reading a recipe or fixing a bike with greasy hands, you ask for the next step and a unit conversion entirely by voice, the screen-free speaker holding your place so you never touch a phone.
